svg.title: Shell and terminal connected through a PTY pair
svg.desc: Retro green-on-black diagram. Left: terminal emulator with an inner screen showing a shell prompt. Center: kernel column with /dev/ptmx (master file), line discipline, /dev/pts/N (slave file). Right: bash process with three fd pins on its left edge for stdin, stdout, stderr. A thick cable runs from the terminal to /dev/ptmx. Three wires run from the shell's fd pins to /dev/pts/N. stdin's arrow points into the shell. stdout and stderr arrows point out of the shell into the slave file.
svg.text: wire = open file descriptor . shell sees only files, never the screen . terminal sees only the master, never the shell
